skill.name: lark-vc-agent
skill.description: Lark video conference agent: let a bot join or leave an in-progress meeting on behalf of the current user, and read real-time events during the meeting (participant join/leave, speech, chat, screen sharing, and more). Provides three shortcuts: +meeting-join, +meeting-events, and +meeting-leave.
skill.summary: AI bot that joins Lark video conferences, reads real-time meeting events, and leaves on command.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All 389 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation files (SKILL.md and 3 reference files) that show examples of lark-cli commands in code blocks. The external_commands findings are shell backtick examples inside markdown documentation, not executable code. The filesystem findings are relative path cross-references between skill documentation files (e.g., ../lark-shared/SKILL.md). The network findings are legitimate URLs to Lark support resources. The obfuscation findings are false positives caused by Chinese character text having higher entropy. This is an official skill from larksuite that provides a controlled CLI interface for video conference bot automation.

content.user_title: Join and monitor Lark video conferences with an AI bot
content.value_statement: Users cannot have an AI assistant attend meetings and track real-time events on their behalf. This skill lets a bot join, observe, and leave Lark video conferences, providing event timelines of participants, chat messages, screen sharing, and transcription.
actual_capabilities: Join an in-progress Lark video conference using a 9-digit meeting number
Leave a meeting using the meeting ID obtained from the join command
Read real-time meeting events including participant join/leave, chat, transcription, and screen sharing
Filter events by time range and continue pagination using page tokens
Preview API calls with --dry-run before executing write operations
Output events in human-readable pretty format or structured JSON format
limitations: Bot can only read events for meetings it is currently in or was in within 5 minutes of the meeting ending
Cannot access participant lists, notes, or transcripts of past meetings (use lark-vc skill instead)
Only supports user identity and requires prior authentication via lark-cli auth login
Cannot forcibly remove other participants or control meeting settings

use_cases.title: Automated meeting monitoring
use_cases.description: A bot joins a meeting to record real-time events including who joined and left, what was said in chat, when screen sharing started, and transcription snippets.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads and project managers who need meeting attendance records

use_cases.title: Proxy meeting attendance
use_cases.description: When a user cannot attend a meeting, they send a bot on their behalf. The bot joins, observes the discussion, and reports back key events and shared content.
use_cases.target_user: Busy professionals who need meeting coverage when unavailable

use_cases.title: Meeting assistant automation
use_cases.description: A bot joins alongside the user to help track shared documents, action items, and discussion flow in real time, then summarizes the findings.
use_cases.target_user: Knowledge workers who want AI-assisted meeting support

prompt_templates.title: Join a meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Join the Lark video conference with meeting number 123456789 on my behalf.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple join request with a valid 9-digit meeting number

prompt_templates.title: Check meeting activity
prompt_templates.prompt: I am in the meeting with ID 6912345678912345678. Who has joined and left so far? What is being discussed in chat?
prompt_templates.scenario: User is already in a meeting and wants a status update

prompt_templates.title: Leave a meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Please leave the meeting with ID 6912345678912345678. I no longer need the bot there.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to end the bot meeting session

prompt_templates.title: Full meeting workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Join the Lark meeting 987654321, monitor the discussion for 10 minutes, report who attended and what was shared, then leave the meeting.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-step workflow combining join, monitor, summarize, and leave

output_examples.input: Join the meeting 123456789 and tell me what is happening.
output_examples.output: I have joined the meeting "Weekly Engineering Sync" (ID: 6912345678912345678). Current status: 5 participants, meeting has been running for 12 minutes. Latest events include 2 participants joining and 1 chat message from Alice about the deployment timeline.

output_examples.input: What has happened in the meeting since I last checked?
output_examples.output: Since your last check 30 seconds ago: 3 new events recorded. Participant Bob joined the meeting. A document titled "Q3 Roadmap" was shared. A chat message from Carol reads: "The new API is ready for review."

best_practices: Always verify the 9-digit meeting number source before joining to avoid entering the wrong meeting
Record the meeting.id returned by +meeting-join immediately as it is required for all subsequent operations
Use --dry-run to preview API calls before executing write operations when unsure about parameters
anti_patterns: Do not use +meeting-join when only querying meeting data - the bot should only join when explicit attendance is needed
Do not reuse old event results to answer questions about an ongoing meeting - always fetch the latest event stream
Do not pass a 9-digit meeting number to +meeting-events or +meeting-leave - these commands require the long numeric meeting ID

faq.question: What is the difference between lark-vc and lark-vc-agent?
faq.answer: lark-vc handles post-meeting queries such as search, participant snapshots, and notes. lark-vc-agent handles in-meeting actions: bot join, real-time events, and bot leave.

faq.question: What is the meeting ID and why is it different from the meeting number?
faq.answer: The meeting ID is a long numeric identifier returned by +meeting-join. It is different from the 9-digit meeting number. Use the meeting ID for +meeting-leave and +meeting-events commands.

faq.question: Can the bot join a meeting that has not started yet?
faq.answer: No. The bot can only join meetings that are currently in progress. It cannot join meetings that have not started or have already ended.

faq.question: Is the bot visible to other meeting participants?
faq.answer: Yes. Joining is a real write operation. The bot appears in the participant list and other attendees can see it join and leave.

faq.question: Can the bot read events from past meetings?
faq.answer: Only within 5 minutes after the meeting ends. After that window, use lark-vc with +notes or +recording to access meeting records.

faq.question: Does the bot need authentication before use?
faq.answer: Yes. The user must authenticate first using lark-cli auth login with user identity. The bot only supports user identity.
